title: RoutingConfig
description: Full routing configuration for a workflow type. DOMAIN INVARIANT: every work item must be routed to a group — an unrouted work item is not permitted. Structurally enforced: defaultGroup is required and cannot be omitted. Rules are evaluated in ascending priority order; first match wins. Condition tree supports arbitrary nested AND/OR logic via LEAF/GROUP nodes.

workflowType.description: Links this config to WorkItems of this type.

defaultGroup.description: REQUIRED. The resolution group all work items are routed to when no rule matches. validateConfigs verifies this references an active ResolutionGroup. Structurally required by JSON Schema — validateConfigs rejects any routing config without this field.

alertOnDefault.description: When true, falling back to defaultGroup fires an alert via the observability stack and increments the routing.fallback.total counter.

rules.description: Ordered routing rules. Evaluated in ascending priority order; first match wins. An empty array means all items route to defaultGroup.

priority.description: Evaluation order. Lower number = evaluated first. validateConfigs warns on duplicate priorities.

conditions.description: Recursive condition tree. Supports arbitrary nested AND/OR logic. A LEAF node tests a single field; a GROUP node combines children with AND or OR.

targetGroup.description: Resolution group id to assign when this rule matches. validateConfigs verifies this references an active ResolutionGroup.

active.description: Inactive rules are skipped during evaluation.

effectiveFrom.description: Optional ISO-8601 date from which this rule is active. Null means active immediately.

effectiveTo.description: Optional ISO-8601 date after which this rule is inactive. Null means no expiry.

createdBy.description: userId of the user who created this config version.

oneOf.title: LeafCondition
oneOf.description: Tests a single field against a value.

field.description: Dot-notation path into WorkItem.fields (e.g. counterparty.region, trade.notionalAmount.amount). Resolved by FieldPathResolver.

operator.description: GT/GTE/LT/LTE use BigDecimal comparison for MONETARY and DECIMAL fields, as declared in field-type-registry. EXISTS tests whether the field path is present regardless of value.

value.description: Comparand. For IN/NOT_IN, this must be an array. For MONETARY comparisons, provide the amount string (e.g. '1000000.00'). For EXISTS, value is ignored.

oneOf.title: GroupCondition
oneOf.description: Combines child conditions with AND or OR logic. Children can be LEAF or GROUP nodes, enabling arbitrary nesting. Example: (region=EMEA AND amount>1M) OR flagged=true.
